info.title: eros-engine API
info.description: Open-source AI companion engine — chat + 6-dim affinity + memory. Source of truth: src/routes/.

get.summary:
Latest user-turn affinity delta (post-EMA). For per-turn FE observation.
NOT behind EXPOSE_AFFINITY_DEBUG (the FE owns this surface); still JWT +
ownership checked.

post.summary:
Cold-mount bundle: resolves (or creates) the session and returns its slim
history in one round-trip (collapses the FE's start + history calls).
post.description:
Affinity is intentionally NOT bundled here: the FE reads affinity on its
own (full values via its DB middleware; per-turn deltas via
`/bff/v1/comp/affinity/{sid}/event`). This keeps bootstrap independent of
`EXPOSE_AFFINITY_DEBUG` — turning that gate off does not change this shape.

400.description: missing genome_id and no existing instance

get.summary:
Slim chat history for the chat-screen mount. Same auth, same ownership
check, same `limit ∈ [1, 50]` clamp as `/comp/.../history`. **Intentional
divergence:** default `limit=50` (canonical defaults to 20). Reason:
BFF exists for cold-mount where the FE wants a full backscroll in one
round-trip.

Inspect the live affinity vector for a session. The session must be
owned by the JWT user; otherwise 403.

Paginated affinity-event log for a session — pre-EMA + post-EMA deltas.
Debug-gated (same router as get_affinity).

post.summary: Start (or resume) a chat session for the JWT user.
post.description:
Resolution rules:
  * `instance_id` provided → must belong to the JWT user.
  * else `genome_id` provided → look up (or auto-create) the user's
    active instance of that genome.
  * else (neither provided) → 400 Bad Request.

400.description: missing genome_id and no existing instance

403.description: instance not owned by this user

get.summary: Paginated chat history (oldest-first) for the given session.

All sessions for the JWT user. The `{user_id}` path parameter MUST
match the JWT's user_id; mismatch returns 403.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Owner user id (must equal JWT sub)

get.summary:
Companion insights + computed training level for the JWT user. The
path `user_id` MUST match the JWT's user_id; mismatch returns 403.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Owner user id (must equal JWT sub)

AffinityDeltasDto.description:
Mirror of `eros_engine_core::affinity::AffinityDeltas` with `ToSchema`
for OpenAPI emission. Field-for-field conversion both ways.

deltas.description: Pre-EMA raw delta the pipeline decided.

oneOf.description: Post-EMA effective change (after − before). None for pre-0014 rows.

Post-EMA delta folded into the two lines (raw-composite units). None for
pre-0014 rows with no `effective_deltas`.

event_id.description: Stable unique id of this event (FE freshness/dedup key).

oneOf.description: Per-turn tier transition; absent when no tier moved.

total.description: Count returned in this page (== events.len()), NOT the grand total.

AffinitySnapshot.description:
Point-in-time projection of a session's `Affinity`. Same field set
as the historical `AffinityDebugResponse`; renamed because it now
flows through non-debug surfaces too.

effective_deltas.description:
Post-EMA effective change of the latest user-turn event. All-zero for
a ghost turn (AI didn't reply; no axis moved).

oneOf.description:
Exact per-turn bond/chemistry delta (floored), read from the stored event
column. None on pre-migration rows.

event_id.description: Stable unique id of this event — the FE freshness/dedup key.

oneOf.description: Engine-authoritative per-turn tier transition; absent when no tier moved.

None when no user-turn event yet (brand-new, or only time_decay), or
when the latest user-turn event predates migration 0014.

client_msg_id.description:
Client-supplied message id forwarded during streaming (the
`client_msg_id` the FE sent on send). NULL for rows that never carried
one, e.g. assistant turns. Lets the FE reconcile an optimistic local
message with its persisted row.

id.description: Stable message id — the `engine.chat_messages` row primary key (UUID).

tips_amount_usd.description:
Structured tip amount for `role='gift_user'` rows. Omitted from
response when None (`skip_serializing_if`). Lets clients render tips
at the right point in the timeline without parsing the `(打赏 $X)`
content marker. Spec:
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-26-tip-role-and-filter-audit-design.md §3.4.

total.description:
Count of `messages` in this response (== `messages.len()`). NOT the
total row count for the session — pagination doesn't know how many
rows remain. Mirrors `HistoryResponse::total`.

history_limit.description:
History page size for the bundled history. Default 50, capped at 50.
BFF-only field; not present in the canonical /comp/chat/start body.

history.description: Most-recent N messages, oldest-first. Empty for brand-new sessions.

BondChemistryDeltas.description:
One turn's exact per-turn bond/chemistry line delta, computed at persist time
from the floored before/after scores and read from the stored event column.

DrawImageRequest.description:
Body of the engine draw endpoint. Echoes the `image_request` frame contents
back plus the draw-time knobs. The engine draws `composed_prompt` verbatim —
no re-compose, no persona load, no persistence.

composed_prompt.description:
base64(STANDARD) of the final wire prompt — exactly the `composed_prompt`
from the `image_request` frame. Decoded and drawn verbatim.

image_ref.description: Which reference image the plan chose (`face` | `previous`).

message_id.description:
The assistant message id `X` from the `image_request` frame. Echoed on
every draw frame so the consumer correlates the draw to its bubble.

ImageReplyParams.description:
Per-turn image parameters. Presence of this block signals the consumer
handles images this turn (it drives image-action availability). The engine
composes the prompt and emits a single `image_request` frame — it never
draws on the chat stream. Draw-time knobs (model, reference URLs, resolution)
live on the draw endpoint (`DrawImageRequest`), not here.

LabelTransitionDto.description:
One line's tier transition (serialised keys). Read-side mirror of
`eros_engine_core::affinity::LabelTransition`.

LlmAuditDto.description:
Caller-supplied OpenRouter audit passthrough. All three fields are
optional; engine never inspects content. See `docs/llm-audit.md`.

metadata.description:
Up to 16 string-valued key/value pairs. Key regex
`^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]{1,64}$`, value `chars ≤ 512`.

session_id.description:
Caller-defined session / conversation grouping. Distinct from the
URL path's `session_id`. `chars ≤ 256`. Forwarded as wire
`session_id`.

user.description:
Free-form caller identifier (recommended: hash of internal user id).
`chars ≤ 256`. Forwarded as OpenRouter wire `user`.

tag.description: ASCII identifier, regex `^[a-z0-9_]{1,32}$`. Used for logging.

text.description:
Verbatim text inserted under `[additional_guidance]` in the system prompt.
1 ≤ chars ≤ 2000 after trim.

genome_id.description: Optional genome id. Required when `instance_id` is absent.

instance_id.description:
Optional explicit instance id. If absent, the server picks (or
auto-creates) the user's instance for the supplied genome.

is_demo.description:
Mark the new session as a demo. Persisted to `metadata.is_demo` and
read by the affinity pipeline to apply `demo_ema_inertia` instead
of the global value, so meters move visibly within the turn budget.
Ignored when resuming an existing session.

StreamPreErrorBody.description:
Pre-stream error body per spec §1.3. Schema-only struct for utoipa;
runtime renders the same shape via StreamPreError.

reply_to_message_id.description:
Optional id of a `chat_messages` row in this session to anchor context on.
When valid, history rewinds to (and includes) that message; when present
but unresolvable, history is dropped for this turn (recorded in metadata).

TurnLabelChangesDto.description:
Per-turn tier transition across the two lines, read from the stored
`companion_affinity_events.label_changes` JSONB.

tags.name: bff-companion
tags.description: Frontend-shaped mirror of /comp/* for first-party clients; shape may diverge from canonical without notice
